named_technique: Adamczewski-Bugeaud digit complexity + prime-gap injection density
bridge_lemma: Rationality of S forces eventual P-periodicity of its binary digits (d_k). The block d_{N+1}..d_{N+L} with L=⌊log_2 p_{N+1}⌋+1 equals the binary digits of p_{N+1}, up to a uniformly bounded carry from Σ_{k≥2} p_{N+k}/2^k. Periodicity then turns the (N+1)-th prime into a deterministic function of constantly many earlier primes, contradicting prime-binary diversity Θ(2^L/L) from Guth-Maynard 2024.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (digit setup). Write S = 0.d_1 d_2 d_3 ... in binary. Assume for contradiction S = a/b ∈ Q. Then (d_n) is eventually periodic with some period P ≥ 1 starting from some position N_0. Step 2 (truncation). For each N ≥ 1, write S = T_N + R_N where T_N = Σ_{k=1}^{N} p_k/2^k and R_N = Σ_{k>N} p_k/2^k. The tail R_N is in (0, p_{N+1}/2^N + p_{N+2}/2^{N+1} + ...) = O(N log N / 2^N) by PNT. So R_N · 2^N = p_{N+1} + p_{N+2}/2 + p_{N+3}/4 + ... ∈ (p_{N+1}, p_{N+1} + p_{N+2}). Step 3 (digit-block identification). The digit block d_{N+1} d_{N+2} ... d_{N + ⌊log_2 p_{N+1}⌋ + 1} is determined by the binary representation of ⌊R_N · 2^N⌋ + carry, where carry ∈ {0, 1} comes from the lower-order fractional sum Σ_{k≥2} p_{N+k}/2^k. By Bertrand p_{N+2} ≤ 2 p_{N+1} and the geometric decay, this fractional sum is at most 2p_{N+1}/2 = p_{N+1}, so carry is bounded (it can be 0 or 1; more precise: the integer part of R_N · 2^N differs from p_{N+1} by at most 1 ≪ p_{N+1}). Step 4 (periodicity prediction). By eventual periodicity, d_{N+j} = d_{N+j-P} for all j ≥ 1 and N ≥ N_0. So the binary block (d_{N+1}, ..., d_{N+L}) with L = ⌊log_2 p_{N+1}⌋ + 1 is determined by the digits at positions N - P + 1 through N - P + L. Step 5 (prime-block content). On the other hand, the same block equals (the binary digits of p_{N+1}) up to a bounded carry. Therefore the binary representation of p_{N+1} is determined (up to carry-bounded perturbation) by digits at positions N - P + 1 to N - P + L, which themselves come from p_{N - P + 1}'s injection. Iterating, p_{N+1} is determined (up to bounded carries) by p_{N+1-P}, p_{N+1-2P}, ... down to p_{N_0+1}. Step 6 (contradiction via Guth-Maynard). The map p_{N+1} ↦ p_{N+1-P} via 'bounded-carry-equality' is a finite-to-one predictor: it allows at most O(1) values of p_{N+1} per (p_{N+1-P}, p_{N+1-2P}, ...) tuple. But by Guth-Maynard 2024 (arXiv:2405.20552), the number of primes in intervals of length x^{17/30 + o(1)} is asymptotic to the prime-counting expectation, and in particular the number of distinct primes p with floor(log_2 p) = m is approximately 2^m / (m log 2). Since m = ⌊log_2 p_{N+1}⌋ ~ log_2(N log N), the number of length-m binary patterns realized by primes grows like 2^m / m, vastly exceeding any finite-state bound O(1)^P from periodicity. Contradiction. Step 7 (irrationality). The contradiction in Step 6 shows that no rational a/b can equal S, so S is irrational.
candidate_lemmas: carry_bounded_injection: For every N ≥ 1, the integer part ⌊R_N · 2^N⌋ where R_N = Σ_{k > N} p_k / 2^k satisfies |⌊R_N · 2^N⌋ - p_{N+1}| ≤ 1. (Use p_{N+2} ≤ 2 p_{N+1} and Σ_{k≥2} 2^{1-k} = 1.)
digit_block_equals_prime_binary: The binary digits d_{N+1} ... d_{N+⌊log_2 p_{N+1}⌋+1} of S coincide with the binary digits of ⌊R_N · 2^N⌋, hence with the binary digits of p_{N+1} ± 1.

periodicity_forces_finite_state_predictor: If (d_n) is eventually P-periodic from N_0, then for every N ≥ N_0 the block (d_{N+1}, ..., d_{N+L}) is a deterministic function of (d_{N+1-P}, ..., d_{N+L-P}).
prime_binary_pattern_diversity: By Guth-Maynard (arXiv:2405.20552), the number of distinct length-m binary patterns realized by primes p with ⌊log_2 p⌋ = m is Θ(2^m / m) as m → ∞.

finite_state_vs_pattern_diversity_contradiction: A finite-state P-periodic predictor produces at most c^P distinct length-L outputs for some absolute constant c, contradicting Θ(2^L/L) prime-binary diversity once L ≫ P.
rationality_implies_eventual_periodicity: For any S ∈ Q ∩ (0,1), the binary digit sequence of S is eventually periodic (standard Mathlib lemma Real.toBaseDigits_periodic_of_rat or similar).
honest_disclaimer: fit_score=0.42 reflects that the Adamczewski-Bugeaud digit-complexity lane is a genuinely new attack vector vs. the prior 2-adic integrality attempts (slots 699/704/1219, all compiled_no_advance), but the bridge_lemma is novel and not yet established in any verified paper. The fusion lane CAN: hand Aristotle a reformulation that converts the irrationality of Σ p_n/2^n into a finite-state predictor non-existence claim about prime binary patterns, leveraging the carry-bounded injection lemma (which is a clean Lean target via Mathlib's existing prime/binary infrastructure). The fusion lane CANNOT pre-verify that Step 6's quantitative gap between O(1)^P periodicity and Θ(2^m/m) prime binary diversity is provable WITHOUT a further input on prime distribution mod 2^L that is not in Guth-Maynard verbatim — this is the genuinely open piece. Mathlib has Nat.Prime, Nat.nth, binary digit lemmas, but NO Adamczewski-Bugeaud subword complexity, NO Guth-Maynard zero-density, NO prime-binary diversity bound. Aristotle would either need to formalize a self-contained variant of the contradiction or, more realistically, reduce E251 to a NEW intermediate lemma about Lebesgue-density of prime binary patterns. This is genuinely a fresh angle — orthogonal to multiplicative-function correlation (E257), to lacunary β-expansion (E267), and to bare 2-adic carries (slots 699/704/1219).
